records.ety: [L. praecludere, praeclusum; prae before + claudere to shut. See Close, v.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Precluded; p. pr. & vb. n. Precluding.]

defs: To put a barrier before; hence, to shut out; to hinder; to stop; to impede.
quotes: The valves preclude the blood from entering the veins.

defs: To shut out by anticipative action; to prevent or hinder by necessary consequence or implication; to deter action of, access to, employment of, etc.; to render ineffectual; to obviate by anticipation.
quotes: This much will obviate and preclude the objections.
